title: Ponte de Rialto
tags: venice italy venetian italia city historic travel outdoors landmarks water rialto rialtobridge pontederialto europe bridge market grandcanal nikon
description: The Rialto Bridge is the oldest of the four bridges spanning the Grand Canal in Venice, Italy. Connecting the sestieri of San Marco and San Polo, it has been rebuilt several times since its first construction as a pontoon bridge in 1173, and is now a significant tourist attraction in the city. The first dry crossing of the Grand Canal was a pontoon bridge built in 1181 by Nicolò Barattieri. It was called the Ponte della Moneta, presumably because of the mint that stood near its eastern entrance.[4] The development and importance of the Rialto market on the eastern bank increased traffic on the floating bridge, so it was replaced in 1255 by a wooden bridge.[4] This structure had two ramps meeting at a movable central section, that could be raised to allow the passage of tall ships. The connection with the market eventually led to a change of name for the bridge. During the first half of the 15th century, two rows of shops were built along the sides of the bridge. The rents brought an income to the State Treasury, which helped maintain the bridge. Maintenance was vital for the timber bridge. It was partly burnt in the revolt led by Bajamonte Tiepolo in 1310. In 1444, it collapsed under the weight of a crowd rushing to see the marriage of the Marquis of Ferrara[3] and it collapsed again in 1524. The idea of rebuilding the bridge in stone was first proposed in 1503. Several projects were considered over the following decades. In 1551, the authorities requested proposals for the renewal of the Rialto Bridge, among other things. Plans were offered by famous architects, such as Jacopo Sansovino, Palladio and Vignola, but all involved a Classical approach with several arches, which was judged inappropriate to the situation. Michelangelo also was considered as designer of the bridge. The present stone bridge, a single span designed by Antonio da Ponte, began to be constructed in 1588 and was completed in 1591. It is similar to the wooden bridge it succeeded. Two ramps lead up to a central portico. On either side of the portico, the covered ramps carry rows of shops. The engineering of the bridge was considered so audacious that architect Vincenzo Scamozzi predicted future ruin. The bridge has defied its critics to become one of the architectural icons, and top tourist attractions, in Venice. Thanks for taking a look. description: The Winged Lion is the biblical symbol of St Mark, patron of Venice.  The Evangelist's relics lie in the city's cathedral having been relocated from Alexandria in 828.  This was to fulfil his angelic dream of a visitation by a winged lion exclaiming that his body would rest in Venice.  He is depicted across the city holding a Bible, like here in the Rialto Fish Market staircase.

title: Loyalty
tags: streetphotography fuji travel juanaround xt30 fujifilm explore juancarlosgomez venice italy mercado lealtad market europa italia colour loyalty viaje trip color perro europe dog
description: Loyalty Does loyalty come from dependence? Do fully free people be dependable? If you float you are free, if you are in the ground, you are stable. Some people say, "if you want to go fast, go alone, if you want to go far go with a mate". Loyalty is what puts you between another person and a bullet, it is effort and challenging work. It is a commitment. It is "no matter what, we are in this together". What are you without loyalty?  Lealtad ¿La lealtad proviene de la dependencia? ¿Son confiables las personas totalmente libres? Si flotas eres totalmente libre, si estás en el suelo eres estable. Algunas personas dicen: "si quieres ir rápido, ve solo, si quieres llegar lejos, ve con un compañero". La lealtad es lo que te pone entre otra persona y una bala, es esfuerzo y trabajo duro. Es un compromiso. Es "pase lo que pase, estamos juntos en esto". ¿Qué eres sin lealtad?

Some people say, "if you want to go fast, go alone, if you want to go far go with a mate". Loyalty is what puts you between another person and a bullet, it is effort and challenging work. It is a commitment. It is "no matter what, we are in this together". What are you without loyalty?  Lealtad ¿La lealtad proviene de la dependencia? ¿Son confiables las personas totalmente libres? Si flotas eres totalmente libre, si estás en el suelo eres estable. Algunas personas dicen: "si quieres ir rápido, ve solo, si quieres llegar lejos, ve con un compañero". La lealtad es lo que te pone entre otra persona y una bala, es esfuerzo y trabajo duro. Es un compromiso. Es "pase lo que pase, estamos juntos en esto". ¿Qué eres sin lealtad?

description: Italy, Venice, near Ponte Rialto, the lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without his patina, the gondolas, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side.  The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.  The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, the first & original "Harry’s Bar", opened by Giuseppe Cipriani in Venice in March 1931, named after ”Harry Pickering” who gave Cipriani, a Bartender at the Hotel Europe, the first money to open his own bar, today there are numerous franchises worldwide,   Guiseppe Cipriano is also the creator, around in the forties, of the classic long drink “Bellini”, marrow from sun-ripened pink peaches & Prosecco Spumante the drink is simple but great & at 22€, 2010, a cocktail highly overpriced like all other cocktails at Harris Bar too.  By the way, the “Beef Carpaccio”, created at the five star hotel Cipriani & owner of the “Harry’s Bar”, still served in the original way, not thinly sliced on a meat slicing machine, rather the raw beef tenderloin is flattened between foil, seasoned with a creamy kind of mayonnaise dressing & served with a small side salad at over 60 € is also absolutely highly overpriced, even so it was good, ... in general food & beverage is tasty & well prepared,…  however, prices are in no relation to quality & value,…  well,... "Hemmingway's Ghost" makes  it possible.  📌... If it would not have been for Hemmingway, who choose this place as his favoured bar in Venice as his hangout & consequently also by many other famous customers from culture, art, show business/Hollywood & business “joint the club”. Just to mention a few like Charlie Chaplin, Princess Aspasia of Greece, Alfred Hitchcock, Jimmy Stewart, Truman Capote, Arturo Toscanini, Orson Welles, Baron Philippe de Rothschild, Aristotle Onassis, Barbara Hutton, Peggy Guggenheim, Woody Allen, George Cloone & today mainly by simple tourists like me.  My main reason to visit the Harry’s Bar was simply because of my curiosity for food & beverage & gastronomical historically well-known locations. Without its past heritage it would be just another simple corner bar without any special flair & a service attitude like, "just another tourist-voyeur we will not see again", seems they don’t care about a good tip for adequate service.  Anyway, ... I was there, I saw & felt the "atmosphere",  it was not good, it was not bad,....it was just the "opposite", ⦂⚪)… cheers…🍸,  ... like "Alfredos" restaurant in Rome, the creator of "Fettuccine Alfredo", I came, I saw & I'm enriched now for one more "one-time experience" & that's enough, "Veni, Vidi, Effugit",  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Möllevångstorget Market Square in Möllevången, a district of Malmö the capital of Scania, in Øresund, Sweden. The market square Möllevångstorget and the surrounding former working-class district Möllevången in Malmö is nowadays probably the most prominent example of Malmö’s multicultural charm. A colourful fruit and vegetable market is held on the Möllevångstorget every day. In the surrounding house are smaller shops with a variety of goods from around the world. In the evening, a vibrant nightlife awakes – nowhere in Malmö is the concentration of exotic restaurants, pubs and bars higher than in the so-called district "Mölle". Möllevången is sometimes even referred to as the Soho of Malmö. Information Source: www.guidebook-sweden.com/en/guidebook/destination/moellev...

The construction of the "Vajoliroja" had a cost of around € 29 million.   “Vajoliroja”, the 47,55 mtr long, in 1920s neo-classic style charter yacht was built 2001in Turkey by Turquoise Yacht Construction in Turkey. After been refitted in 2008/9 & later renamed “Amphitrite”. Bestowing cabins for a maximum of 10 guests staying on board, the Vajoliroja accommodates everyone comfortably. This ship has 7 to 9 proficient crew members to maintain & sail, taking care of the guests in 5 staterooms, including the elegant & spacious master suite, located forward on the main deck, with an adjoining study area & bathroom in suite, 1VIP stateroom, 1 double cabin, four cabins are situated on the lower deck comprising of three double / twin cabins, an upper & lower bedded cabin with additional Pullman berth.  A 7 mtr custom built Riva-style tender stowed forward on the main deck ensuring uncluttered after deck areas for guests to enjoy the al fresco dining in optimum privacy & comfort. description: Italy, Venice, "Marala", a classic 1930s luxury motor yacht, integrated with the latest in modern entertainment, safety & navigation systems. The home port is Piraeus, Greece, but official flagged in Panama. The accommodation capacity offering seven comfortable cabins for a maximum of 13 yacht guests spending the nights & under normal conditions she has room for approx. 18 expert crew members to operate the yacht. The steel hull, aluminium & steel superstructure yacht is 58.8 mtr long, a beam of 8.08 mtr, a deep draught of almost 4 mtr, two 750 Horse Power main engines, reaching a high speed of 15 knots  = 27.78 km built with a teak wood deck by Camper & Nicholsons ship building company in Southampton, United Kingdom  📌 ....if you still have some 10 million++€ lying around somewhere & don't need it, you can buy the Marala with it, …just for nice weekends, photo shootings or so. title: Rialto Markets, Erberia (Fruits & Vegetables)
tags: venice rialto erberia sanpolo travel market rialtobridge pescheria vegetables produce rivoaltus italy outdoor venezia shopping stall
description: From the western end of the Rialto Bridge (in Venice, Italy, San Polo-Rialto neighborhood) a short walk takes you straight into the heart of Venice's two main markets....the 'Pescheria' selling fish, and the 'Erberia' where stalls display an inviting array of fruits and vegetables.  Rialto was the first inhabited zone of Venice and was named in Latin 'Rivoaltus' or 'high bank' which was the actual name of the city until the year 1000 when it became Venezia.  It is also the smallest area of the San Polo district and people tended to live 'over the shop' as their home and workplace were combined.

title: Rialto Markets - Pescheria (Fish Market)
tags: market venice italy travel rialtobridge fish sanpolo pescheria erberia stall merchant people food vendor
description: From the western end of the Rialto Bridge (in Venice, Italy, San Polo neighborhood) a short walk takes you straight into the heart of Venice's two main markets....the 'Pescheria' selling fish, and the 'Erberia' where stalls display an inviting array of fruits and vegetables. The actual 'pescheria' or covered fish market was once Venice’s public slaughter house before it gained a reputation for fish.

description: Italy, Venice, at the "Giardini della Biennale", …Big Brother is watching.  The area of parkland in the historic city of Venice which hosts a major part of the city's cultural Biennale Art Festival.  A light breeze of spicy sea air from the Adriatic, the whisper of the leaves in the trees & bushes, no sound of the vaporetti, water taxis & barges. This park not far from the city centre, on the eastern edge of Venice is a green oasis of relaxation not only for the Venetians. An exception is, when like every two years, the cultural event "La Biennale Art Exhibitions" takes place between May & November.  The gardens were created by Napoleon Bonaparte who drained an area of marshland in order to create a public garden on the banks of the Bacino di San Marco. The traditional site of La Biennale Art Exhibitions since the first edition in 1895, the Giardini were made by Napoleon at the beginning of the nineteenth century. It was the success of the first editions to trigger the building of foreign pavilions since 1907, which were added to the already built Central Pavilion. The Giardini now host 29 pavilions of foreign countries, some of them designed by famous architects.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Church of Santa Maria Zobenigo, also known as Chiesa di Santa Maria del Giglio. A baroque Roman Catholic Church built by Giuseppe Sardi for Admiral Antonio Barbaro between 1678 & 1681. The church has one of the finest Venetian Baroque facades in all of Venice.   The facade lacks any Christian image statues or reliefs. It shows the marble relief maps of various places in which Antonio Barbaro served, also including his own statue, in the centre, other statues are his brothers.  Along the nave are painted depictions of the Via Crucis or Stations of the Cross by various artists, the nave ceiling is decorated with a large canvas by Antonio Zanchi. The Molin chapel contains a Madonna & Child, the only painting by the Flemish painter Rubens in Venice. The altar has flanking statues, behind the high altar, in the sanctuary are paintings of several famous Italian painters.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondoliere.Traffic.01.041023.4627.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, …a gondolier needs all his skills to maneuver his gondola through Venice`s  narrow waterways & traffic. There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side.  The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.  The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Giardini della Biennale", an area of parkland in the historic city of Venice which hosts a major part of the city's cultural Biennale Art Festival.  A light breeze of spicy sea air from the Adriatic, the whisper of the leaves in the trees & bushes, no sound of the vaporetti, water taxis & barges. This park not far from the city centre, on the eastern edge of Venice is a green oasis of relaxation not only for the Venetians. An exception is, when like every two years, the cultural event "La Biennale Art Exhibitions" takes place between May & November.  The gardens were created by Napoleon Bonaparte who drained an area of marshland in order to create a public garden on the banks of the Bacino di San Marco. The traditional site of La Biennale Art Exhibitions since the first edition in 1895, the Giardini were made by Napoleon at the beginning of the nineteenth century. It was the success of the first editions to trigger the building of foreign pavilions since 1907, which were added to the already built Central Pavilion. The Giardini now host 29 pavilions of foreign countries, some of them designed by famous architects.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, view over the top of the “Torre dell’Orologio”, the clock tower at the Piazza di San Marco. The two great bronze figures strike each full hours on the bell. One statue represents an old person, the other one a young person, to symbolise the transitory of time. They sculptures are acknowledged as "the Moors" because of the dark patina acquired by the bronze. The bell is also original & is signed by one Simeone who cast it at the Arsenal in 1497.  The Tower & its large Astronomical Clock, a masterpiece of technology & engineering, form an essential part of the Venice image & the archway has been for over 500 years one of the most famous architectural landmarks in Venice, erect over an arch that leads into the main shopping street of the city, the old Merceria & to the Rialto. title: Phot.Venice.Gondoliere.Traffic.01.041023.4627.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, …a gondolier needs all his skills to maneuver his gondola through Venice`s  narrow waterways & traffic. There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side.  The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.  The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, …for a bird's eye view, a total lack of respect by the pigeons for the 2000 years old lion at the entrance to the “Arsenale”.  The Arsenale used to be originally Venice’s shipyard, founded in 1104. Nowadays it belongs to the Italian marine corps & not accessible to the public, the only exception is every two years during the “Biennale”, the oldest established forum for contemporary art.  The arsenal was surrounded by walls & towers like a fortress, to which there were only two entrances; the water gate, "Ingresso all'Acqua", which is flanked by two towers erected in 1574 & if necessary, could be closed off by a gate. Through this gate, shipping traffic was handled over the harbour basin into the Mediterranean.  The land gate, portal gate - "Ingresso di Terra". The Land Gate is located next to the Water Gate, which in its current form was built around 1460 in the manner of a triumphal arch to show that Venice is famous for its wealth & fortune. The entrance stairs are flanked by 8 symbolic figures. The two giant ancient lions to the right & left of the stairs are loot pieces that Francesco Morosini brought from Greece to Venice in 1687. The left three-meter-high lion had been near Athens in Porto Leone for two thousand years.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. title: Phot.Venice.Market.Fish.01.041027.5804.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto", the market near Ponte Rialto displays Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto", Palombo, Mustelus mustelus, the small houndshark can reach a maximum length of 160 cm, more frequently individuals of this species measure 60-90 cm. The houndshark is viviparous, the up to 30 embryos develop completely in the mother's body & the young are born after a pregnancy of about 10 months with a birth length around 35 cm. The shape closely resembles that of the classic shark, the skin or checkering is relatively smooth compared to that of other sharks; the colouring is usually variable of a uniform gray on the back & clear ventrally.   The meats are good & Palombi is highly appreciated compared to those of other sharks; so it can happen that other inferior quality species similar in shape & size sold once the skin & head are cut for Palombi, therefore the head on the real Palombi is always kept on the clean fish. description: Italy, Venice, oarswomen training for the “Vogalonga”, the long row is a game & a protest. The 32 kilometres rowing regatta starts at St. Mark’s Basin, moves towards the islands of Vignole, Sant´Erasmo & Burano, the northern lagoon through Murano, the Rio Cannaregio, on to the new Venetia, then continues past the Palazzi of the Grand Canale & finishes at the Punta della Dogana, opposite St. Mark's Square. The famous Vogalonga regatta was founded in Venice in 1974, when a group of friends decided to hold a regatta, taking a stand against the increasing use of motorboats, the deterioration of the city & to celebrate Venetian traditions. The event takes place each year on a Sunday in May, when for a few hours the suffocating traffic of motorized boats ceases & the water is left alone to the rowers. The one day event, the 39th Vogalonga takes place in 2013 on Sunday, May 19. At the first official regatta, on May 8 1975, 500 boats from Venice & neighbouring cities took part, each year the regatta has grown in size & now over 1500 boats with about 6000 challengers participate each year in this colourful & spectacular sportive happening for all kinds, sizes & shapes of rowing boats.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, Campo de la Beccarie at the Mercato del Pesce al Minuto near Ponte Rialto. Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondolas.01.041023.4633.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga
description: Italy, Venice, Gondolas parked at Fondamento Orseo, next to San Marcos square. There are officially 425 Gondolas in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the Family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  The oar-paddle, which is several meters long, lies in a fork, forcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side. To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style. The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, the “Caffè Florian” under the arcades of the “Procuratie Nuove” at the San Marco Square was founded by the Cafétier Floriano Francesconi in December 1720 under the name "Venezia Trionfante", Triumphant Venice, but the Venetian only called the Café by the first name of the owner & so it was changed later into “Caffè Florian”.   The Café used to be the place to meet for Italy’s patriots, artists & intellectuals as well as international well-known personalities; today’s patrons are basically tourists.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the venetian buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, without their unique buildings & their patina, the gondolas, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canale Grande; these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice,…I think.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondolas.01.041023.4633.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga
description: Italy, Venice, Gondolas parked at Fondamento Orseo, next to San Marcos square. There are officially 425 Gondolas in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the Family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  The oar-paddle, which is several meters long, lies in a fork, forcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side. To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style. The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga
description: Italy, Venice, “Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute”, commonly known simply as “La Salute”, is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Botoli * Violet artichoke
tags: botoli carciofi market artichoke violetartichoke venice venezia italy
description: The castraura (the first artichoke to appear) is cut in advance to allow the plant to develop other 18-20 artichokes called botoli, delicious but not as prestigious as their brother. They grow a little all over the lagoon (Vignole, Lio Piccolo, Malamocco, Mazzorbo), but the best come along with the island of Sant’Erasmo, the old orchard of Venice, whose vegetables are fed in part by a sandy soil, chalky in part.

tags: nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode piazza square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia italy venice venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga santamariadellasalute torredell’orologio clocktower
description: Italy, Venice, “Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute”, commonly known simply as “La Salute”, is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Clock.Tower.01.041028.6075.jpg
tags: nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode piazza square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia italy venice venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga santamariadellasalute torredell’orologio clocktower
description: Italy, Venice, view over the top of the “Torre dell’Orologio”, the clock tower at the Piazza di San Marco. The two great bronze figures, which strike each full hours on a bell. One is old & the other young, to show the transitory of time, they are acknowledged as "the Moors" because of the dark patina acquired by the bronze. The bell is also original & is signed by one Simeone who cast it at the Arsenal in 1497.  Below this level is the winged lion of Venice with the open book, before a blue background with gold stars, originally a statue of the Doge Agostino Barbarigo who was kneeling before the lion, but in 1797, after the city had capitulated to Napoleon, the Doge was removed by the French, who were eliminating all symbols in the city of the old regime.  The Tower & its large Astronomical Clock, a masterpiece of technology & engineering, form an essential part of the Venice image & the archway has been for over 500 years one of the most famous architectural landmarks in Venice, erect over an arch that leads into the main shopping street of the city, the old Merceria & to the Rialto.  👉 One World one Dream, ...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

tags: nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode piazza square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia italy venice venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga santamariadellasalute torredell’orologio clocktower
description: Italy, Venice, view over the top of the “Torre dell’Orologio”, the clock tower at the Piazza di San Marco. The two great bronze figures, which strike each full hours on a bell. One is old & the other young, to show the transitory of time, they are acknowledged as "the Moors" because of the dark patina acquired by the bronze. The bell is also original & is signed by one Simeone who cast it at the Arsenal in 1497.  Below this level is the winged lion of Venice with the open book, before a blue background with gold stars, originally a statue of the Doge Agostino Barbarigo who was kneeling before the lion, but in 1797, after the city had capitulated to Napoleon, the Doge was removed by the French, who were eliminating all symbols in the city of the old regime.  The Tower & its large Astronomical Clock, a masterpiece of technology & engineering, form an essential part of the Venice image & the archway has been for over 500 years one of the most famous architectural landmarks in Venice, erect over an arch that leads into the main shopping street of the city, the old Merceria & to the Rialto.  👉 One World one Dream, ...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Santa Maria della Salute, the Roman Catholic Church is commonly known simply as “La Salute” on the Grand Canal, to be found at Punta della Dogana in the Dorsoduro. The Dom is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.  👉 One World one Dream, ...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  7,5 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, the Canal Grande at twilight. The last "S" shaped, almost 4 km of the river Brenta is Venice's main waterway, the Canal Grande, over 40 smaller canals flow into the Grand Canal.  There are currently four bridges spanning the canal, the oldest of which is the Rialto Bridge, a covered limestone bridge built at the end of the 16th century instead of a wooden bridge. Until the Scalzi Bridge & Accademia Bridge, built in the 19th century, the Rialto Bridge was the only permanent link across the canal. In addition to the bridges, the urban ferries, "Vaporetti" & gondolas, operating only during daytime, allow to cross the Grand Canal in eight places.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over 13 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Palazzo Cavalli-Franchetti, the palace next to the Ponte dell'Accademia & the Palazzo Barbaro on the Grand Canal. The palace erected in 1565, served during the centuries several famous & rich owners as home & since 1999 the seat of the “Istituto Veneto di Scienze Lettere ed Arti”. In the 19th century the palace was internally modernised & the façade with rich window framing in Venetian Gothic style enriched.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondolas.St.Marcus.01.041028.5980
tags: nikon d800 d300 coolpix square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market venedig venezia venise murano burano cathedral opera brasserie
description: Italy, Venice, The “Lion of Venice” with its approximate weight of 3 tons surmounts one of two large granite columns in the Square “Piazzetta di San Marco” facing the gondolas on the lagoon.  Believed to have been erected about 1268 carrying ancient symbols of the two patron saints of Venice. The eastern column bears a creature which at some point came to represent the “Lion of Saint Mark”, traditional symbol of the evangelist. The sculpture has had a very long & obscure history, probably starting its existence as a winged lion-griffin statue on a monument to the god “Sandon” at Tarsus in Cilicia, Southern Turkey about 300 BC. Executions of declared death sentences were carried out between the freestanding columns in the piazzetta next to the lagoon. Even today Venetians won't walk between the two columns; a final chance to live was given if you could walk around the edge of the third column on the seaward side of the palace, …no one ever succeeded.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, “Piazzetta di San  Marco”with its two columns facing the gondolas on the lagoon. The “Lion of Venice” with its approximate weight of 3 tons surmounts one of two large granite columns in the Square, thought to have been erected about 1268 carrying ancient symbols of the two patron saints of Venice. The eastern column bears a creature which at some point came to represent the “Lion of Saint Mark”, traditional symbol of the evangelist. The sculpture has had a very long & obscure history, probably starting its existence as a winged lion-griffin statue on a monument to the god “Sandon” at Tarsus in Cilicia, Southern Turkey about 300 BC. Executions of declared death sentences were carried out between the freestanding columns in the piazzetta next to the lagoon. Even today Venetians won't walk between the two columns; a final chance to live was given if you could walk around the edge of the third column on the seaward side of the palace, …no one ever succeeded.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over 16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondolas.Night.01.041023.4642.jpg
tags: barcelona china plaza venice españa india paris france streets berlin salzburg me valencia germany munich münchen square de champselysees austria calle spain nikon frankreich opera asia strada place cathedral market louvre frankfurt zurich hamburg eiffeltower chinese beijing gear places montmartre historic notredame strasbourg curacao concorde bombay and mumbai rue alexandre murano venise venezia francia venedig konstanz spanien brasserie burano centregeorgespompidou défense d800 arche d300 trocadéro sacrécœur strasen plätze my iii” “arc triomphe” “pont “nikonflickraward” flickrstruereflection1
description: Italy, Venice,  …it's raining, it's pouring, a gondliere life can be boring…,  Gondolas parked at Fondamento Orseo, next to San Marcos square. There are officially 425 Gondolas in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the Family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  The oar-paddle, which is several meters long, lies in a fork, forcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side. To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style. The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: San Polo is the smallest of the six sestieri of Venice, northern Italy, covering 86 acres (35 hectares) along the Grand Canal. It is one of the oldest parts of the city, having been settled before the ninth century, when it and San Marco formed part of the Realtine Islands. The sestiere is named for the Church of San Polo. The district has been the site of Venice's main market since 1097, and connected to the eastern bank of the Grande Canal by the Rialto bridge since the thirteenth century. The western part of the quarter is now known for its churches, while the eastern part, sometimes just called the Rialto, is known for its palaces and smaller houses. Attractions in San Polo include the Rialto Bridge, the Church of San Giacomo di Rialto (according to legend the oldest in the city), the Campo San Polo, the House of Goldoni, the Church of Santa Maria Gloriosa dei Frari, the Church of San Rocco and the Scuola Grande di San Rocco.

title: Swimming in Alici (Anchovies)
tags: venice wine market fishmarket venezia rialto anchovy foodie alici vegetablemarket dalluva
description: These babies are everywhere at the Rialto Fish Market.  Young, succulent anchovies, called Alici (ah-LEE-chee) in Venice and Acciughe (ah-CHOO-gay) elsewhere, are a mainstay throughout the coastal regions of Italy.  In Venice you’ll find them grilled; personally I like them flash fried in olive oil, like they make them in Genoa – fresh, crisply, salty little “fries with eyes” that you pop in your mouth, head and all.

title: Canocce, the oddball shrimp
tags: venice wine market shrimp fishmarket venezia rialto foodie gambero vegetablemarket dalluva canocce
description: OK, these buggers look like something out of prehistoric times.  A fellow travel buddy of mine always says, “the uglier the fish, the better it tastes.”  That must go for crustaceans as well.  These centipede-looking “shrimp” are the sweetest, most succulent I have ever tasted.  Local trattorie serve them by the plateful.

title: Castraure (baby Artichokes) to die for
tags: venice wine market fishmarket venezia artichoke rialto foodie carciofi vegetablemarket dalluva castraure
description: The best of the best, baby artichokes are called "castraure" (pronounced cass-trah-OO-ray, and literally translate as "castrated") and are the most tender. They’re the side shoots of the plant, nipped young and before the fuzzy choke has developed. You boil/braise/steam these babies, drizzle a little olive oil or butter on them, and pop the whole thing in your mouth.

title: Many varieties of Radicchio
tags: venice wine market fishmarket venezia rialto foodie radicchio vegetablemarket dalluva
description: Italians love their radicchio, and they grow an immense variety. My favorite is the speckled Radicchio Castelfranco.  Note the phrase “nostrano” used to denote truly local produce, grown by the fruttivendolo herself.  There is so much more to the Rialto fruit and vegetable market, which has it’s own seasons and rituals.  You’ll have to experience it for yourself on your next visit – a must-do itinerary item while in Venezia.
